response:
1. to find the equilibrium population size $ P_e $, we set $ \frac{dP}{dt} = 0 $, which gives:

$$
rP\left(1 - \frac{P}{K}\right) - h(t) = 0
$$

substituting the given values $ r = 0.1 $, $ K = 1000 $, and $ h(t) = 10 + 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right) $:

$$
0.1P\left(1 - \frac{P}{1000}\right) - 10 - 5\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}t\right) = 0
$$

solving this equation for $ P $ gives the equilibrium population size $ P_e $.

2. under the new model, the carrying capacity decreases linearly with time, $ K(t) = 1000 - 3t $. the stability of $ P_e(t) $ depends on the sign of the derivative $ \frac{dP_e}{dt} $. if $ \frac{dP_e}{dt} > 0 $, the population is increasing; if $ \frac{dP_e}{dt} < 0 $, the population is decreasing. the time when the population will no longer be sustainable is when $ P_e(t) \leq 0 $.
